content: Citation style: APA 7th edition. Always include the DOI when available; for preprints, link the arXiv abs URL (not the PDF). Don't cite blog posts in the formal lit review — they go in a separate appendix file.
summary: APA 7th, DOI required, arXiv abs (not PDF), no blog posts in main lit review.

content: Arushi's active research area: agent memory architectures. Reading list this quarter: retrieval-augmented generation, mechanistic interpretability, agent memory architectures. Lit-review scope is post-2022 LLM-era only — older work goes in the historical-context appendix.
summary: Research area: agent memory. Reading list: RAG, mech interp, agent memory. Scope: post-2022 LLM-era.

content: Working hypothesis: structured, typed memory with deterministic ranking outperforms naïve RAG on multi-session evaluation tasks. Key claims: (a) provenance enables eval reproducibility, (b) recency × kind-priority handles temporal validity better than cosine similarity. Empirically supported by the multi-session-recall experiment (2026-04-28): Statewave 0.81, naïve RAG 0.34, history-stuffing 0.62.
summary: Hypothesis: structured + deterministic memory > naïve RAG. Empirically: 0.81 vs 0.34 vs 0.62 (April 28 experiment).

content: Sole co-author for the active NeurIPS submission: Dr. Mei Wu (UC Berkeley, PT timezone). Sole co-author after the MIT collaborator declined in November 2025. Mei prefers async review — comments on shared drafts, no live calls unless flagged urgent.
summary: Co-author: Dr. Mei Wu (UCB, PT). Async review only. (MIT collab declined Nov 2025.)

content: Key papers cited in the lit review so far: Park et al. 2024 'Generative Agents' (DOI 10.1145/3586183.3606763) — memory-stream scoring, retrieval-time LLM grading vs Statewave's compile-time grading. Chen 2025 'Survey of Memory Architectures for Autonomous Agents' (arXiv:2501.04822) — episodic/semantic/procedural/working taxonomy, used to frame section 2.
summary: Cited: Park 2024 (Generative Agents, retrieval-time grading), Chen 2025 (memory taxonomy survey).

content: Active draft: 'Scoring Memories with Provenance: A Determinism-First Approach to Agent Memory' (NeurIPS 2026 target). Sections done: 1 (intro), 2 (related work), 4 (architecture). Section 3 (formalisation) settled on hard temporal-validity cutoffs for cleaner determinism proofs; sigmoid decay relegated to a 'Practical extensions' subsection. Mei reviewed and agreed (2026-04-15).
summary: Draft: NeurIPS 2026. Done: §1, §2, §4. §3 = hard cutoffs (Mei agreed 2026-04-15). Sigmoid decay in subsection.

content: Tooling stack: Zotero for citations + BibTeX export, Obsidian for first-pass notes, Markdown drafts converted to LaTeX via pandoc. Don't suggest Mendeley or Notion — both break the pandoc pipeline.
summary: Tools: Zotero + BibTeX, Obsidian, Markdown → LaTeX via pandoc. NOT Mendeley/Notion.

content: Notebook style: explicit imports (no `from x import *`), no auto-format on save (autopep8 / black both reflow list comprehensions in ways Arushi finds harder to read). Run formatters only at submission time.
summary: Explicit imports, no save-time auto-format. Format only at submission.

content: Experiment 'compiler-density' (2026-02-25): heuristic vs LLM compilation on a 200-episode synthetic operational dataset. Heuristic ~5 memories per 100 episodes, recall@5 0.42. LLM ~10 memories per 100 episodes, recall@5 0.71. LLM is ~2× density and ~1.7× recall on operational data — justifies the cost.
summary: compiler-density (Feb 25): LLM 2× density, 1.7× recall vs heuristic on operational data.

content: Key deadlines: NeurIPS 2026 abstract due 2026-08-15, full paper 2026-08-22. Internal milestones: section 3 done mid-May, full draft to Mei by 2026-07-01, four-week revision window. ICLR 2026 camera-ready (separate paper, 'Provenance-Bound Retrieval', accepted as poster) due 2026-04-30.
summary: NeurIPS abstract 2026-08-15, paper 2026-08-22. Draft to Mei 2026-07-01. ICLR camera-ready 2026-04-30.

content: ICLR 2026: 'Provenance-Bound Retrieval' (separate paper, same research vein) — accepted as poster on 2026-03-26. Reviewer 2 swayed by added vector-baseline comparison; reviewer 3 marked intro restructure as 'much improved.' Camera-ready due 2026-04-30.
summary: ICLR 2026: Provenance-Bound Retrieval accepted as poster (2026-03-26). Camera-ready 2026-04-30.

content: Core argument for NeurIPS paper section 1: ranking determinism implies eval reproducibility. If (subject, task, budget) always returns the same context bundle, retrieval regression tests are well-defined. Vector-only systems can't make that claim because index state varies. This is the wedge against naïve RAG.
summary: Section 1 wedge: deterministic ranking → reproducible eval; vector-only ≠ deterministic.

content: Reading queue (priority order, target completion 2026-04-25): (1) Liu et al. 2025 on subject-design patterns for multi-tenant agent memory, (2) Voigt 2026 preprint on temporal validity in semantic memory, (3) Tanaka 2025 on idempotent compilation. Citations to be incorporated into section 2.
summary: Reading queue: Liu 2025 (subject design), Voigt 2026 (temporal validity), Tanaka 2025 (idempotent compilation). Done by 2026-04-25.

content: Personal habit: two 90-minute focused reading blocks each morning. No meetings before 11:00 PT. Email and Slack closed during reading blocks — reviewed at 11:30. Don't suggest synchronous pings or meetings during the morning window.
summary: Mornings = focused reading (2× 90m). No meetings before 11:00 PT.

summary: Section 3 was completed ahead of schedule with all subsections finalized.

content: Run completed confirming the hypothesis that deterministic ranking produces monotonic accuracy.
summary: The experiment confirmed the hypothesis regarding deterministic ranking accuracy.

content: Mei's feedback indicated that section 3 is now publication-ready with suggestions for figure 4.
summary: Mei confirmed section 3's readiness for publication with minor suggestions.

content: Section 4.1 first draft completed, building on previous experiments.

content: Voigt 2026's provenance-bound retrieval is a re-derivation of last year's ICLR paper.
summary: Voigt 2026's work is based on a previous ICLR paper.

content: Mei approved subsections 3.1 and 3.2, but flagged 3.3 for being too implementation-specific.
summary: Mei provided feedback on subsections of Section 3.

content: Park & Singh 2026's memory-stream scoring extends to 1M-memory subjects via approximate k-NN.
summary: Park & Singh's work on memory-stream scoring is relevant for scaling.

summary: Arushi declined a co-author invitation to maintain focus on NeurIPS.

summary: The ICLR 2026 poster has a submission deadline.

content: Chen et al. 2026's eval harness is the first published one for multi-session agent recall.
summary: Chen et al.'s work is significant for multi-session agent recall evaluations.

content: Reading-block schedule is set for every weekday from 09:00-10:30 PT.
summary: A consistent reading-block schedule has been established.

content: Compiler-density experiment (2026-02-25, 200-episode synthetic dataset): heuristic compiler extracted ~5 memories per 100 episodes; LLM compiler extracted ~25 per 100. The 5x density difference drove the decision to default Northwind onto STATEWAVE_COMPILER_TYPE=llm.
summary: Compiler-density experiment: heuristic ~5/100, LLM ~25/100 memories.

content: Citation format for preprints: link the arXiv abs URL (not the PDF). Always include the DOI when available. APA 7th edition for the formal bibliography.
summary: Preprint citation: arXiv abs URL + DOI when available, APA 7th.
